title: Merrick Bank — Charge-off Tradeline Dispute (TransUnion · Experian · Equifax)
source: TransUnion credit report pull — June 6, 2026 (via AnnualCreditReport.com)
notes: CHARGE-OFF SINCE APPROX. 2022. Credit limit: $550. Reported balance: $1,064 (193% utilization). Merrick Bank has likely profited significantly from this account — years of interest/fee revenue collected pre-charge-off, tax deduction claimed on charged-off balance, and probable debt sale to third party at commercial profit. Despite full monetization, continues to report an inflated over-limit balance across all three bureaus causing ongoing, compounding harm. Dispute grounds: (1) balance exceeds credit limit by 93% without itemization — FCRA § 623(a)(8); (2) credit limit omitted/inaccurate distorting utilization; (3) continued reporting after profit extraction lacks legitimate purpose; (4) method of verification demanded under FCRA § 611(a)(7). Relief sought: delete tradeline OR correct balance to verified, itemized amount. Dispute letters sent to: TransUnion, Experian, Equifax (bureau disputes) + Merrick Bank direct (furnisher dispute). DRAFT ONLY — external action locked pending Isiah Howard approval.
legal_theories: FCRA § 611 — failure to conduct reasonable reinvestigation
FCRA § 623(a)(8) — furnisher accuracy obligation, over-limit balance without itemization
leverage_notes: Account charged off ~2022. Approx. 4 years of post-charge-off continued reporting. Furnisher has extracted full commercial value (interest/fee revenue + tax write-off + probable debt sale). Continued inaccurate reporting with zero remaining legitimate commercial purpose is strong FCRA willful-noncompliance argument under § 1681n.
next_action: Isiah Howard to review and approve before any submission
